channel.title: Posts on Le Cahier
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Le Cahier

item.title: First Post in this Blog
item.description: After many months considering it, and even started it but chickened because of the seeming complexities of it, I finally got around to putting this simple blog together. This site is powered by Hugo and edited completely in Rstudio. I still marvel at R these days. Doing statistics is one thing, finite difference is another, but making a package so that people can blog using R? I’ll continue writing here, mostly small notes, quotes, and some codes.

item.title: Weekend R Project - Flixbus' Stops in Switzerland
item.description: Having been avid users of Flixbus for three years now, I and my wife know very well the importance of knowing where the Flixbus stop of our destination is. And not only for Flixbus, but also for other modes of mass transportations, such as train, tram, or subways. Traditionally, before departing for traveling using Flixbus, we always gathered all the addresses of the bus stops that we’re going to go to.

item.title: First Leaflet Test
item.description: This is my first blog post featuring a leaflet map. I’ve followed the step-by-step guide provided in this blog post and apparently it works. To say that I’m happy would be an understatement. Now I’m able to write blog post with maps embedded in it. I’m going to look up how to “beautify” this website though; perhaps I’m going to find some custom themes. God knows how bad I am with CSS.

item.title: First Blog Post in Github Pages: Finally
item.description: After many months considering it, and even started it but chickened because of the seeming complexities of it, I finally got around to putting this simple personal website together. Powered by Jekyll and Github Pages, this site is much easier to made and much simpler to maintain than my old Wordpress blog. It’s also a lot easier to set up than I thought it was going to be. It’s amazing. Currently, it’s still in development, of course, but stay tuned!
